summary:
The adult spinal cord generally terminates around the lower border of the first lumbar vertebra or sometimes as high as the upper margin of the second lumbar vertebra. Key variations exist but are rare. To summarize:

The adult spinal cord usually reaches down to the lower border of the first lumbar vertebra, often extending slightly higher to just above the second lumbar vertebra. Variations do occur but are uncommon.
